articles.title: Thanksgiving
articles.author: Mary Foster Gibbs
articles.complete_text:
THANKSGIVING.
By Mary Foster Gibbs.

For all Thy providences, God of Peace and War,
I thank Thee!
Thank Thee for storms on my uncovered, cringing head?
For tears and pain?
For all the awful clang and whirr of cruel war?
Say that again!

For all Thy providences, God of Peace and War,
I thank Thee!
Thank Thee for life, for motherhood, for work, for death.
For my free will,
The power to climb from deeps to heights, while earth and I
Both learn to trust Thee still.

For all Thy providences, God of Peace and War,
I thank Thee!
articles.notes: This is a complete Thanksgiving poem by Mary Foster Gibbs that appears in the contents but was not extracted as a standalone file. It's a substantial poetic work with clear authorship and thematic content appropriate for the November 1918 issue.

misc_summary: The remaining content consists of legitimate miscellaneous material including: magazine masthead/publication information, table of contents, general board member listings, extensive advertisements for local Salt Lake City businesses (garments, printing, jewelry, undertakers, schools, etc.), and subscription promotional material for the Relief Society Magazine.
